assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'All 4 slices restart simultaneously — TPU multislice environments don't support rolling updates' and 'TPU multislice maintenance is always atomic'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Clearly states 'Cascading restart trigger — maintenance on ANY single slice forces restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: States 'Complete pod termination — all training pods across all 1,024 chips will be terminated and recreated' and 'Your training job will experience complete interruption'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: States 'If insufficient TPU v5e resources exist during recreation, slices will block until hardware becomes available — this could extend downtime significantly'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Multiple references including 'Checkpoint current training state immediately' and 'Force checkpoint now (don't wait for scheduled checkpoint)'

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Provides 'Post-Maintenance Validation' section with restart commands and mentions 'Plan training resume process post-maintenance' and checkpoint resume validation

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud command for 30-day maintenance exclusion and discusses 'Maintenance exclusion: Apply a 30-day no upgrades exclusion to defer until a planned training gap'

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Mentions 'Coordinated timing: Work with your Google contact to schedule during your planned checkpoint window' and discusses scheduling considerations
